sargas.title: Sarga 26
sargas.introduction: In this chapter Seetha ponders a lot and decides to give up life without Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसक्ताश्रुमुखी त्वेवं ब्रुवती जनकात्मजा |
अधोगतमुखी बाला विलप्तुमुपचक्रमे || ५-२६-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. prasaktaashrumukhii = with a face continually shedding tears; jankaatmajaa 
= Seetha; balaa = an young woman; evam = thus; bruvatii = speaking; adhogatamukhii 
= with downward face; upachakrame = started; vilaptum = to weep.

verses.sanskrit:
उन्मत्तेव प्रमत्तेव भ्रान्तचित्तेव शोचती |
उपावृत्ता किशोरीव विवेष्टन्ती महीतले || ५-२६-२

राघवस्य प्रमत्तस्य रक्षसा कामरूपिणा |
रावणेन प्रमथ्याहमानीता क्रोशती बलात् || ५-२६-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. raaghavasya = (while) Rama; pramattasya = was heedless; aham 
= I; kroshatii = who have been crying; balaat aaniitaa = (was) forcibly brought; pramathya 
= overpowered; rakshasaa = by the ogre; raavaNena = Ravana; kaamaruupiNaa = who can 
wear desired form.
verses.translation:
"While Rama was heedless (taken away by Mareecha), I who have been crying was forcibly brought 
overpowered by the ogre Ravana who can wear desired form."

verses.sanskrit:
राक्षसीवशमापन्ना भर्त्स्यमाना सुदारुणम् |
चिन्तयन्ती सुदुःखार्ता नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे || 
५-२६-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. aapannaa = obtaining; raaskasii vasham = control by ogresses; bartsyamaanaa 
= being frightened; sudaaruNam = very horribly; chintayantii = thinking; suduHkhaartaa 
= being tormented by grief; aham = I; na utsahe = do not desire; jiivitum = to live.
verses.translation:
"Being under the control of ogresses, being frightened very horribly, thinking and being tormented 
by grief, I do not desire to live."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि मे जीविते नार्थो नै वार्थैर्न च भूषणैः |
वसन्त्या राक्षसीमध्ये विना रामं महारथम् || 
५-२६-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. vinaa = without; raamaM = Rama; mahaaratham = a great car-warrior;
vasantyaaH = living; raakshasii madhye = in the midst of ogresses; arthaH na = there 
is no use; jiivitena = with life; me = to me; na arthaiH = not with wealth; na 
bhushhaNaishcha = not with ornaments.
verses.translation:
"Without Rama, a great car-warrior, living in the midst of ogresses, there is no use with life 
to me; not with wealth, not with ornaments."

verses.sanskrit:
अश्मसारमिदं नूनथवाप्यजरामरम् |
हृदयं मम येनेदं न दुःखेनावशीर्यते || ५-२६-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. athavaa = or; mama = my; idam = this; hR^idayam = 
heart; nuunam = truly; ashmasaaram = (is made of) iron; ajaraamaramapi = is undecaying 
and without death; yena = by what reason; naavashiiryate = is it not destroying; duHkhena 
= with sorrow.
verses.translation:
"Or my this heart truly is made of iron. It is undecaying and without death. By what reason is 
it not destroying with sorrow?"

verses.sanskrit:
धिङ्मामनार्यामसतीं याहं तेन विना कृता |
मुहूर्तमपि रक्षामि जीवितं पापजीविता || ५-२६-७
verses.translation:
"Whoever I with a sinful life made to be without Rama, protecting life even for a moment, to such 
me who am ignoble, who am bad wife, shame on me."

verses.sanskrit:
का च मे जीविते श्रद्धा सुखे वा तं प्रियं विना |
भर्तारं सागरान्ताया वसुधायाः प्रियंवदम् 
|| ५-२६-८
verses.translation:
"Without husband Rama who is the lord of the earth with ocean at the end, a pleasant talker, what 
is the interest to me in life or in comfort."

verses.sanskrit:
भिद्यतां भक्ष्यतां वापि शरीरं विसृजाम्यहम् |
न चाप्यहं चिरं कुःखं सहेयं प्रियवर्जिता || 
५-२६-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. aham = I; visR^ijaami = will leave; shariiram = the body;
bhidyataam = let it be split (into pieces); bhakshyataam vaapi = or let it be eaten; aham 
= I; priyavarjitaa = without husband; na cha saheyam = will not tolerate; duHkham = 
sorrow; chiram = for a long time.
verses.translation:
"I will leave the body. Let it be split into pieces or let it be eaten. I without husband will 
not tolerate sorrow for a long time."

verses.sanskrit:
चरणेनापि सव्येन न स्पृशेयं निशाचरम् |
रावणं किं पुनरहं कावयेयं विगर्हतम् || ५-२६-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. aham = I; na spR^isheyam = will not touch; savyena charaNenaapi 
= even with my left foot; raavaNam = Ravana; nishaacharam = ogre; vigarhitam = of reprehensible 
conduct; kaamayeyam kiM punaH = what to say of desiring (him).
verses.translation:
"I will not touch even with my left foot Ravana an ogre of reprehensible conduct. What to say of 
desiring him."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रत्याख्यातं न जानाति नात्मानं नात्मनः कुलम् |
यो नृशंसस्वभावेन मां प्रार्थयितुमिच्छति 
|| ५-२६-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. yaH = whoever (Ravana); nR^ishaMsa svabhaavena = with a cruel nature;
icchhati = is desiring; praarthayitum = to solicit; maam = me (that Ravana); na 
jaanaati = does not know; aatmaanam = him; pratyaakhyaatam = to be refused; na 
= (he does) not (know); aatmanaH = his; kulam = race.
verses.translation:
"Whoever Ravana with a cruel nature is desiring to solicit me, that Ravana does not know him to 
be refused; he does not know his race."

verses.sanskrit:
चिन्ना भिन्ना विभक्ता वा दीप्तेवाग्नौ प्रदीपिता |
रावणं नोपतिष्ठेयं किं प्रलापेन वश्चिरम् 
|| ५-२६-१२
verses.translation:
"Even if cut or if divided into parts in blazing fire, I will not reach Ravana. What is the use 
with your prattling for a long time?"

verses.sanskrit:
ख्यातः प्राज्ञः कृतज्ञ्शश्च राघवः |
सद्वृत्तो निरनुक्रोशः शङ्के मद्भाग्यसंक्षयात् || ५-२६-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. raaghavaH = Rama; khyaataH = is famous; praajJNaH = is a wise 
man; saanulroshashcha = is compassionate; sadvR^ittaH = with a good conduct; madbhaagya 
saMkshayaat = due to deterioration of my fortune; saN^ke = I doubt; niranukroshaH = (He 
became) hard hearted.
verses.translation:
"Rama is famous, is a wise man, is compassionate with a good conduct. Due to the deterioration 
of my fortune, I doubt He became hard hearted."

verses.sanskrit:
राक्षसानां सहस्राणि जनस्थाने चतुर्दश |
येनैकेन निरस्तानि स मां किं नाभिपद्यते || ५-२६-१४
verses.translation: "By whom alone fourteen thousand of ogres have been destroyed, why is that Rama not saving me."

verses.sanskrit:
निरुद्धा रावणेनाहमल्पवीर्येण रक्षसा |
समर्थः खलु मे भर्ता रावणं हन्तुमाहवे || ५-२६-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. aham = I; niruddhaa = am withheld; rakshasaa raavaNena = by 
the ogre Ravana; alpaviiryeNa = with little strength; me bhartaa = my husband; samarthaH 
khalu = is indeed capable; hantum = to kill; raavaNam = Ravana; ahave = in a war.
verses.translation:
"I am withheld by the ogre Ravana with little strength. My husband is indeed capable of killing 
Ravana in a war."

verses.sanskrit:
विराधो दण्डकारण्ये येन राक्षसपुङ्गवः |
रणे रामेण निहतः स मां किं नाभिपद्यते || ५-२६-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. yena raameNa = By which Rama; viraadhaH = Viradha; raakshasa puN^gavaH 
= best among ogres; nihataH = has been killed; daNDakaaraNye = in Dandaka forest; raNe 
= in a war; kim = why; saH = (is) that He; naabhipadyate = not saving; maam 
= me.
verses.translation:
"By which Rama Viradha, best among ogres, has been killed in Dandaka forest in a war, why is that 
He not saving me?"

verses.sanskrit:
कामं मध्ये समुद्रस्य लङ्केयं दुष्प्रधर्षणा |
न तु राघवबाणानां गतिरोधो भविष्यति || ५-२६-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. iyam laN^kaa = this city of Lanka; samudrsya madhye = being in the 
middle of ocean; dushhpR^idharshhaNaa kaamam = is difficult to be attacked; tu = but; na 
bhavishhyati = there will not be; gatirodhaH = an obstacle to flying; raaghava baanaaNaam 
= of Rama's arrows;
verses.translation:
"This city of Lanka being in the middle of ocean is difficult to be attacked. But there will not 
be an obstacle to flying of Rama's arrows."

verses.sanskrit:
किं नु तत्कारणं येन रामो दृढपराक्रमः |
रक्षसापहृतां भार्यामिष्टां नाभ्यवपद्यते || ५-२६-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. kim nu = what now; tat kaaraNam = is that reason; yena = by 
which; raamaH = Rama; dR^iDha paraakramaH = with a firm courage; naabhyavapadyate = 
is not protecting; ishhTaam bhaaryaam = His dear wife; apahR^itaam = stolen; rakshasaa 
= by an ogre.
verses.translation:
"What now is that reason by which Rama with a firm courage is not protecting His dear wife stolen 
by an ogre."

verses.sanskrit:
इहस्थां मां न जानीते शङ्के लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः |
जानन्नपि स तेजस्वी धर्षणं मर्षयिष्यति || ५-२६-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. shaN^ke = I doubt that; lakshmana puurvajaH = Rama elder to Lakshmana;
na jaanite = does not know; maam = me; ihasthaam = to be here; jaanannapi 
= after knowing; saH tejasvii = (will) that glorious Rama; marshhyishhyati = tolerate; dharshhaNam 
= (this) outrage.
verses.translation:
"I doubt that Rama elder to Laksmana does not know me to be here. After knowing will that glorious 
Rama tolerate this outrage?"

verses.sanskrit:
हृतेति योऽधिगत्वा मां राघवाय निवेदयेत् |
गृध्रारजोऽपि स रणे रावणेन निपातितः || ५-२६-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. yaH = whoever Jatayu; adhigatvaa = nearing (Rama); nivedayat 
= will have let known; raaghavaaya = to Rama; hR^iteti = (that Seetha) was stolen; saH gR^idhra 
raajaH api = that king of vultures; hataH = has been killed; raavaNena = by Ravana; 
raNe = in war.
verses.translation:
"Whoever Jatayu nearing Rama will have let known to Rama that Seetha was stolen, that king of vultures 
has been killed by Ravana in war."

verses.sanskrit:
कृतं कर्म महत्तेन मां तथाभ्यवपद्यता |
तिष्ठता रावणद्वन्द्वे वृद्धेनापि जटायुषा || ५-२६-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. mahat karma = a great deed; kR^itam = has been done; tena jaTaayushhaa 
= by that Jatayu; tathaa = thus; abhyavapadyataa = protecting; maam = me; vR^iddhenaapi 
= although of old age; tishhThataa = standing; raavaNa dvandve = in a dual combat with Ravana.
verses.translation:
"A great deed has been done by that Jatayu, thus protecting me, although of old age, standing in 
a dual combat with Ravana."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि मामिह जानीयाद्वर्तमानां स राघवः |
अद्य बाणैरभिक्रुद्धः कुर्याल्लोकमराक्षसम् || ५-२६-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. saH raaghavaH = that Rama; jaaniiyaadyadi = if He knows; maam 
= me; iha vartamaanaam = to be living here; abhikruddhaH = with anger; kuryaat = will 
make; lokam = the world; araakshasam = to be without ogres; baaNaiH = with (His) arrows;
adya = now.
verses.translation:
"If that Rama knows me to be living here, with anger He will make the world to be without ogres 
with His arrows now."

verses.sanskrit:
विधमेच्च पुरीं लङ्कां शोषयेच्च महोदधिम् |
रावणस्य च नीचस्य कीर्तिं नाम च नाशयेत् || ५-२६-२३
verses.translation:
"Rama will blow away the city of Lanka, will dry up the great ocean, will destroy the name and 
fame of Ravana."

verses.sanskrit:
ततो निहतनाथानां राक्षसीनां गृहे गृहे |
यथाहमेवं रुदती तथा भुयो न संशयः || ५-२६-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. tataH = thereafter; yathaa = how; aham = I ; evam rudatii 
= am crying in this way; tathaa = in the same way; gR^ihe gR^ihe = in house and house; raakshasiinaam 
= ogresses; nihatanaadhaanaam = with killed husbands (will cry); bhuuyaH = again and again;
na saMshayaH = there is no doubt.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter how I am crying in this way, in the same way, in very house, ogresses with killed husbands 
will cry again and again. There is no doubt in this."

verses.sanskrit:
अन्विष्य रक्षसां लङ्कां कुर्याद्रामः सलक्ष्मणः |
न हि ताभ्यां रिपुर्दृष्टो मुहूर्तमपि जीवति 
|| ५-२६-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. raamaH = Rama; salakshmaNaH = together will Lakshmana; anvishhya 
= will search; laN^kaam = Lanka; rakshasaam = of ogres; kuryaat = will do (destruction);
ripuH = an enemy; dR^ishhTaH = seen; taabhyaam = by those two; na jiivati hi 
= will indeed not live; muhuurtamapi = even for a moment.
verses.translation:
"Rama together will Lakshmana will search the city of Lanka belonging to ogres. An enemy seen by 
those two will indeed not live even for a moment."

verses.sanskrit:
चिताधुमाकुलपथा गृध्रमण्डलसंकुला |
अचिरेण तु लङ्केयं श्मशानसदृशी भवेत् || ५-२६-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. iyam = this; lan^kaa = Lanka; achireNa = shortly; bhavet 
= will become; shmashaana sadR^ishii = like a graveyard; chitaadhuumakulapathaa = with paths 
agitated with smoke of funeral pyres; gR^idhramaNDalasaMkulaa = crowded with a group of vultures.
verses.translation:
"This Lanka shortly will become like a graveyard, with paths agitated with smoke of funeral pyres, 
crowded with a group of vultures."

verses.sanskrit:
अचिरेणैव कालेन प्राप्स्याम्येव मनोरथम् |
दुष्प्रस्थानोऽयमाख्याति सर्वेषाम् वो विपर्ययम् 
|| ५-२६-२७
verses.translation:
"In a short time only I will attain my desire. This evil course is telling of destruction of you 
all."

verses.sanskrit:
यादृशानीह दृशन्ते लङ्कायामशुभानि वै |
अचिरेणैव कालेन भविष्यति हतप्रभा || ५-२६-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. iha = here; laN^kaayaam = in Lanka; yaadR^ishaani = whatever;
ashubhaani = inauspicious things; dR^ishyante = are being seen; achireNaiva kaalena 
= (from those) in a short while; bhavishhyati = (Lanka) will become; hataprabhaa = deprived 
of glory.
verses.translation:
"Here in Lanka whatever inauspicious things are being seen, from those, in a short while Lanka 
will become deprived of glory."

verses.sanskrit:
नूनं लङ्का हते पापे रावणे राक्षसाधमे |
शोषं यास्यति दुर्धर्षा प्रमदा विधव यथा || ५-२६-२९
verses.translation:
"While the sinful, vile ogre Ravana is being killed, Lanka which is unconquerable till now, definitely 
will get drying up like a woman who lost husband."

verses.sanskrit:
पुण्योत्सव समृद्धा च नष्टभर्र्ती सराक्षसी |
भविष्यति पुरी लङ्का नष्टभर्त्री यथाङ्गना || 
५-२६-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. puNyotsava samR^iddhaa = (now) abounding in pious festivities; laN^kaapurii 
= the city of Lanka; nashhTa bhartrii = with lost lord; saraakshasii = together with ogresses;
bhavishhyati = will become; aN^ganaa yathaa = like a woman; nashhTa bhartrii = with 
lost husband;.
verses.translation:
"Now abounding in pious festivities, the city of Lanka with lost lord together with ogresses will 
become like a woman with lost husband."

verses.sanskrit:
नूनं राक्षसकन्यानां रुदन्तीनां गृहे गृहे |
श्रोष्यामि नचिरादेव दुःखार्तानामिह ध्वनिम् || 
५-२६-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. nachiraadeva = in a short while; iha = here; gR^ihe gR^ihe 
= in every house; nuunam shroshhyaami = I can definitely hear; dhvanim = the sound; raakshasakanyaanaam 
= of ogresses; rudantiinaam = crying; duHkhaartaanaam = being tormented by grief.
verses.translation:
"In a short while here in every house I can definitely hear the sound of ogresses crying being 
tormented by grief."

verses.sanskrit:
सान्धकारा हतद्योता हतराक्षसपुङ्गवा |
भविष्यति पुरी लङ्का निर्दग्धा रामसायकैः || ५-२६-३२
verses.translation:
"The city of Lanka will become burnt by Rama's arrows together with darkness, with destroyed luster 
with killed best ogres."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि नाम स शूरो मां रामो रक्तान्तालोचनः |
जानीयद्वर्तमानां हि रावणस्य निवेशने || ५-२६-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. saH raamaH = that Rama; shuuraH = a strong man; raktaanta lochanaH 
= with red eye corners; yadi naama jaaniiyaat = will he know; maam = me; vartamaanaam 
= to be living; niveshane = in the house; raavaNasya = of Ravana.
verses.translation: "Will that Rama who is a strong man with red eye corners know me to be in the house of Ravana."

verses.sanskrit:
अनेन तु नृशंसेन रावणेनाधमेन मे |
समयो यस्तु निर्दिष्टस्तस्य कालोऽयमागतः || ५-२६-३४
स 
च मे विहितो मृत्युरस्मिन् दुष्टे न वर्तते |
verses.wordMeaning:
34. yaH = whatever; samayaH = time; nirdishhTa = ordered; 
anena raavaNena = by this Ravana; nR^ishaMsena = who is cruel; adhamena = (and) vilest 
of men; ayam = this; kaalaH = time; aagataH = has come; me = to me; saH 
mR^ityuH = that death; vihitaH = decreed; me = to me; na vartate = does not hold 
good; asmin dushhTe = in the matter of this evil one.
verses.translation:
"Whatever time has been ordered by this cruel and vilest of men Ravana, this time has come to me. 
That death decreed to me does not hold good to this evil one (Ravana)."

verses.sanskrit:
अकार्यं ये न जानन्ति नैरृताः पापकारिणः || ५-२६-३५
अधर्मात्तु महोत्पातो भविष्यति हि सांप्रतम् 
|
नैते धर्मं विजानन्ति राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः || ५-२६-३६
verses.translation:
"Whoever sinful ogres who do not know what should not be done, due to their unrighteousness now 
there will be a great calamity. These meat eating ogres do not know virtue."

verses.sanskrit:
ध्रुवं मां प्रातराशार्थे राक्षसः कल्पयिस्यति |
साहं कथं करिष्यामि तं विना प्रियदर्शनम् 
|| ५-२६-३७
रामम् रक्तान्तनयनमपश्यन्ती सुकुःखिता |
verses.wordMeaning:
37. raakshsaH = an ogre; dhruvam = will definitely; kalpayishhyati 
= make; maam = me; praataraashaarthe = for breakfast; saa aham = such I; katham 
karishhyaami = what should do; tam vinaa = without Rama; priyadarshanam = with pleasing 
appearance; suduHkhitaa = I am grieving a lot; apashyantii = not seeing; raamam = Rama;
raktaantanayanam = with red eye corners.
verses.translation:
"An ogre will definitely eat me for breakfast. What should such I do without Rama with pleasing 
appearance? I am grieving a lot not seeing Rama with red eye corners."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि कश्चित् प्रदाता मे विषस्याद्य भवेदिह || ५-२६-३८
क्षिप्रं वैवस्वतं देवं पश्येयं पतिना 
विना |
verses.wordMeaning:
38. adya = now; kashchit bhavedyadi = if there exists any one; praadaataa 
= (who is a) giver; vishhasya = of poison; iha = here; patinaa vinaa = (I) without 
husband; kshipram = quickly; pashyeyam = will see; devam = the god; vaivasvatam 
= Yama.
verses.translation:
"Now if there exists any one who is a giver of poison here, I without husband quickly will see 
the god Yama."

verses.sanskrit:
नाजानाज्जिवतीं रामः स मां लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः || ५-२६-३९
जानन्तौ तौ न कुर्यातां नोर्य्वां हि 
मम मार्गणम् |
verses.translation:
"That Rama elder to Lakshmana may not know me to be alive. If those two know me to be alive, then 
it cannot be said that they will not do my search on earth."

verses.sanskrit:
नूनं ममैव शोकेन स नीरो लक्ष्मणाग्रजः || ५-२६-४०
देवलोकमितो यातस्त्यक्त्वा देहं महीतले |
verses.translation:
"That valiant Rama who is the elder brother of Lakshmana due to grief of me, leaving the body on 
earth has gone to celestial world. This is certain."

verses.sanskrit:
धन्या देवाह् सगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः || ५-२६-४१
मम पश्यन्ति ये नाथं रामं राजीवलोचनम् 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
41. ye = whoever;pashyanti = is seeing; mama = my; naatham = husband;
raamam = Rama; raajiiva lochanam = with eyes resembling a lotus flower; devaaH = (such) 
Devas; siddhaashcha = Siddhas; sagandhravaaH = together with Gandharvas; parmarshhayaH 
= and great sages; dhanyaaH = are fortunate.
verses.translation:
"Whoever is seeing my husband Rama with eyes resembling a lotus flower such Devas, Siddhas together 
with Gandharvas and great sages are fortunate."

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा न हि तस्यार्थो द्कर्मकामस्य धीमतः || ५-२६-४२
मया रामस्य राजर्षेर्भार्यया परमात्मनः 
|
verses.translation:
"Or to that Rama with a desire in virtuousness, who is intelligent, a sage-king, the supreme spirit, 
there is no use with me, his wife."

verses.sanskrit:
दृश्यमाने भवेत्प्रीतिः सौहृदं नास्त्यपश्यतः || ५-२६-४३
नाशयन्ति कृतघ्नास्तु न रामो नाशयिष्यति 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
43. priitiH bhavet = friendly disposition will occur; dR^ishyamaane = in 
what is being seen; naasti = there is no; sauhR^idam = friendship; apashyataH = to 
one who does not see; kR^itaghnaaH = ungreatful ones; naashayanti = destroy (friendship);
raamaH tu = but Rama; na naashayishhyati = will not destroy friendship.
verses.translation:
"Friendly disposition will occur in what is being seen. There is no friendship to one who does 
not see. Ungrateful ones destroy frindship. But Rama will not destroy friendship."

verses.sanskrit:
किं नु मे न गुणाः केचित्किं वा भाग्यक्षयो मम || ५-२६-४४
याहं सीदामि रामेण हीना मुख्येन 
भामिनी |
verses.wordMeaning:
44. aham = I; yaa = who; bhaaminii = is a woman; siidaami 
= (who is) perishing; mukhyena raameNa vinaa = without the chief Rama; me = to (such) me;
na kiM nu = are there no; kechit guNaaH = good qualities; kiM vaa = or; mama 
= (has) my; bhaagyakshayaH = fortune diminished.
verses.translation:
"I who is a woman who is perishing without the chief Rama to such me are there no good qualities 
or has my fortune diminished."

verses.sanskrit:
श्र्यो हि हीवितान्मर्तुं विहीनाया महात्मनः || ५-२६-४५
रामादक्लिष्टचारित्राच्छूराच्छत्रुनिबर्हणात् 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
45. vihiinaayaaH = deprived; raamaat = from Rama; aklishhTa chaaritraat 
= with untroubled reputation; shuuraat = a warrior; shatrunibarhaNaat = annihilator of enemies;
mahaatmanaH = a great soul; me = to (such) me; jiivitaat = more than living; martuH 
= death; shreyaH = is good.
verses.translation:
"To me who is deprived of Rama with untroubled reputation, a warrior, annihilator of enemies, a 
great soul, more than living, death is good."

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा न्यस्तशस्त्रौ तौ वने मूलफलाशिनौ || ५-२६-४६
भ्रातरौ हि नरश्रेष्ठौ संवृत्तौ वनगोचरौ 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
46. athavaa = or; tau bhraatarau = those two brothers; narashresThau 
= best among men; nyasta shastrau = giving up weapons; vane = in the forest; saMvR^ittau 
= (may have) become; vanagocharau = dwellers of forest; muulaphalaashanau = eating roots and 
fruits.
verses.translation:
"Or those two brothers who are best among men giving up weapons in the forest may have become dwellers 
of forest eating roots and fruits."

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा राक्षसेन्द्रेण रावणेन दुरात्मना || ५-२६-४७
चद्मना घातितौ शूरौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ 
|
verses.translation:
"Or Rama and Lakshmana the warrior brothers may have been killed by the evil soul king of ogres 
Ravana by deceit."

verses.sanskrit:
साहमेवंगते काले मर्तुमिच्छामि सर्वथा || ५-२६-४८
न च मे विहितो मृत्युरस्मिन् दुःखेऽपि वर्तते 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
48. evam gate = under such circumstances; kaale = (and time); saa 
= such; aham = I; sarvathaa = in all ways; ichchhaami = am desiring; martum 
= to die; asmin dukhaH api = even in this grief; mR^ityuH = death; vihitaH na vartate 
= is not bestowed (on me).
verses.translation:
"Under such circumstances and time, such I in all ways am desiring to die. Even in this grief, 
death is not bestowed on me."

verses.sanskrit:
धन्याः खलु महात्मानो मुनयस्त्यक्तकिल्बिषाः || ५-२६-४९
जितात्मनो महाभागा येषां न स्तः प्रियाप्रिये 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
49. mahaatmanaH = great souled ones; tyakta kilbishhaaH = with abandoned 
sins; jittatmanaH = with a conquered mind; mahaabhaagaaH = those with great fortune; munayaH 
= sages; dhanyaaH khalu = are indeed fortunate; yeshhaam = to whom; na staH = there 
is no; priyaapriye = pleasure or displeasure.
verses.translation:
"Great souled ones with abandoned sins, with a conquered mind, those with great fortune, sages 
are indeed fortunate to whom there is no pleasure and displeasure."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रियान्न संभवेद्दुःखमप्रियादधिकं भयम् || ५-२६-५०
ताभ्यां हि ये वियुज्यन्ते नमस्तेषां महात्मनाम् 
|
verses.translation:
"From pleasure sorrow and from displeasure great fear do not occur to great souls. Whoever are 
separated from pleasure and displeasure, my obeisance to such great souls."

verses.sanskrit:
साहंत्युक्ता प्रियेणैव रामेन विदितात्मना || ५-२६-५१
प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यामि पापस्य रावनस्य 
गता वशम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
51. tyaktaa = left; priyeNaiva raameNa = by dear Rama; viditaatmanaa 
= with a well known mind; vasham gataa = coming under the control; paapasya = of sinful one;
raavaNasya = of Ravana; saa aham = such I; tyakshaami = will leave; praaNaan 
= breath of life.
verses.translation:
"Left by dear Rama with a well known mind coming under the control of sinful Ravana, such I will 
leave breath of life."
verses.commentary:
© July 2005, Kishore Kopalle

    rama and verse

sargas.title: Sarga 27
sargas.introduction:
In this chapter an ogress named Trijata describes her dream. She dreams of Sri Rama triumphs and 
the ogre Ravana is conquered.

verses.sanskrit:
इत्युक्ताह् सीतया घोरं राक्षस्यः क्रोधमूर्छिताः |
काश्चिज्जग्मुस्तदाख्यातुं रावणस्य दुरात्मनः 
|| ५-२७-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. iti = thus; uktaaH = spoken to; siitayaa = by Seetha; raakshasyaH 
= ogresses; krodha muurchhitaaH = swooned with anger; ghoram = terribly; kaashchit 
= some; jagmuH = went; aakhyaatum = to tell; tat = that; raavaNasya = to Ravana;
duraatmanaH = the evil minded one.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः सीतामुपागम्य राक्षस्यो घोरदर्शनाः |
पुनः पुरुषमेकार्थमनर्थार्थमथाब्रुवन् || ५-२७-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. tataH = thereafter; raaksasyaH = ogresses; ghora darshanaaH 
= with a terrible appearance; upaagamya = neared; siitaam = Seetha; atha = then;
punaH = again; abruvan = spoke; anarthaartham = (words of) evil purpose; ekkartham 
= with a single meaning; parushham = (which were) harsh.

verses.sanskrit:
अद्येदानीं तवानार्ये सीते पापविनिश्चये |
राक्षस्यो भक्षयिष्यन्ति मांसमेतद्यथासुखम् || ५-२७-३
verses.translation:
"O ignoble one! O one with a sinful resolve! O Seetha! Today at this moment ogreeses will eat this 
your flesh according to comfort."

verses.sanskrit:
सीतां ताभिरनार्याभिर्दृष्ट्वा संतर्जितां तदा |
राक्षसी त्रिजटा वृद्धा शयाना वाक्यमब्रवीत् 
|| ५-२७-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. tadaa = then; dR^iSTvaa = seeing; siitaam = Seetha; saMtarjitaam 
= frightened; taabhiH anaaryaabhiH = by those evil ones; raakshasii = an ogress; trijaTaa 
= (named) Trijata; shayanaa = sleeping (till then); abraviit = spoke; vaakyam = (these) 
words.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मानं खादतानार्या न सीतां भक्षयिष्यथ |
जनकस्य सुतामिष्टां सुन्नषां दशरथस्य च || ५-२७-५
verses.translation:
"O evil ones! Eat your selves. You will not eat Seetha who is the dear daughter of king Janaka 
and daughter-in-law of king Dasaratha."

verses.sanskrit:
स्वप्नो ह्यद्य मया दृष्टो दारुनो रोमहर्षणः |
राक्षसानामभावाय भर्तुरस्या भवाय च || ५-२७-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. adya = now; svapnaH = a dream; daaruNaH = (which is) terrible;
romaharshhaNaH = causing the hairs to stand erect; abhaavaaya = for the destruction; raakshasaanaam 
= of ogres; bhavaaya = for the welfare; asyaaH bhartuH = of Her husband; dR^iShTaH 
= has been seen; mayaa = by me.
verses.translation:
"Now a dream which is terrible, causing the hairs to stand erect for the destruction of ogres and 
for the welfare of Her husband, has been seen by me."

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्टास्त्रिजटया राक्षस्यः क्रोधमूर्छिताः |
सर्वा एवाब्रुवन्भीतास्त्रिजटां तामिदं वचः 
|| ५-२७-७

कथयस्व त्वया दृष्टः स्वप्नोऽयं कीदृशो निशि |
तासां श्रुत्वा तु वचनं राक्षसीनां मुखाच्य्चुतम् 
|| ५-२७-८
उवाच वचनं काले त्रिजटा स्वप्नसंश्रितम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kiidR^ishii = of what kind; ayam svapnaH = (is) this dream; dR^ishhTaH 
= seen; tvayaa = by you; kathayasva = tell (us); shrutvaa = listening; vachanam 
= (to the) utterance; chyutam = (that) came forth; mukhaat = from the mouths; taasaam raakshasiinaam 
= of those ogresses; trijaTaa = Trijata; uvaacha = spoke; kaale = at that time; 
vachanam = (this) word; svapna saMshritam = relating to the dream.
verses.translation:
"Of what kind is this dream seen by you. Tell us". Listening to this utterance that came forth 
from the mouths of those ogresses, Trijata spoke at that time this word relating to the dream.

verses.sanskrit:
गजदन्तमयीं दिव्यां शिबिकामन्तरिक्षगाम् || ५-२७-९
युक्तां हंससह्रेण स्वयमास्थाय राघवः |

शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरो लक्ष्मणेन सहागतः || ५-२७-१०
verses.translation:
"Rama wearing white garlands and clothes passing through the sky, Himself ascending a palanquin 
made of ivory tied with a thousand swans, arrived along with Lakshmana."

verses.sanskrit:
स्वप्ने चाद्य मया दृष्टा सीता शुक्लाम्बरावृता |
सागरेण परिक्षिप्तं स्वेतं पर्वतमास्थिता 
|| ५-२७-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. adya = now; dR^ishhTaa = (it has been) seen; mayaa = by me;
svapne = in the dream (that); siitaa cha = Seetha also; shuklaambaraavR^itaa = wearing 
white clothes; aasthitaa = was sitting; shvetam parvatam = on a white mountain; parikShiptam 
= surrounded; saagareNa = by the ocean.
verses.translation:
"Now it has been seen by me in the dream that Seetha also wearing white clothes was sitting on 
a white mountain surrounded by the ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
रामेण संगता सीता भास्करेण प्रभा यथा |
राघवश्च मया दृष्टश्च तुर्दंष्ट्रं महागजम् || ५-२७-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. siitaa = Seetha; saMgataa = came together; raameNa = with 
Rama; prabhaa yathaa = like the light; bhaaskareNa = with the Sun; raamashcha = Rama 
also; mayaa dR^ishhTaH = was seen by me; aaruuDhaH = (to be) mounted; mahaagajam = 
on a great elephant; chaturdraShTram = with four tusks; shailasaMkaasham = equalling a mountain;
chachaara = (and) wandered; saha lakshmanaH = together with Lakshmana.
verses.translation:
"Seetha came together with Rama like the light with the Sun. Rama also was seen by me to be mounted 
on a great elephant with four tusks and equalling a mountain and Rama wandered together with Lakshmana."

verses.sanskrit:
ततस्तौ नरशार्दूलौ दीप्यमानौ स्वतेजसा || ५-२७-१३
शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरौ जानकीं पर्युपस्थितौ 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
13. tataH = thereafter; diipyamaanau = shining; svatejasaa = with 
their own radiance; shuklamaalyaambara dharau = wearing white garlands and clothes; tau = those 
two; narashaarduulau = best among men (Rama and Lakshmana); paryupasthitau = neared; jaanakiim 
= Seetha.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter shining with their own radiance, wearing white garlands and clothes, those two best 
among men Rama and Laksmana neared Seetha."

verses.sanskrit:
ततस्तस्य नगस्याग्रे ह्याकाशस्थस्य दन्तिनः || ५-२७-१४
भर्त्रा परिगृहीतस्य जानकी स्कन्धमाश्रिता 
|
verses.translation:
"Thereafter Seetha rested on the upper part of back of elephant in the sky; taken by Her husband 
Rama to the front of that mountain."

verses.sanskrit:
भर्तुरङ्कात्समुत्पत्य ततः कमललोचना || ५-२७-१५
चन्द्रसूर्यौ मया दृष्टा पाणिना परिमार्जती 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
15. tataH = thereafter; mayaa dR^ishhTaa = it has been seen by me (that);
kamalalochanaa = Seetha with eyes like lotuses; samutpatya = has risen up; a~Nkaat 
= from the lap; bahrtuH = of Her husband; parimaarjatii = gently touched; chandra suuryau 
= the Moon and the Sun; paaNinaa = with Her hand.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter it has been seen by me that Seetha with eyes like lotuses has risen up from the lap 
of Her husband and gently touched the Moon and the Sun with Her hand."

verses.sanskrit:
ततस्ताभ्यां कुमाराभ्यामास्थितः स गजोत्तमः || ५-२७-१६
सीतया च विशालाक्ष्या लङ्काया उपरिस्थितः
verses.wordMeaning:
16. tataH = thereafter; saH gajottamaH = that best elephant; aasthitaH 
= ascended; taabhyaam kumaaraabhyaam = by those princes; siitayaa cha = and by Seetha; vishaalakshyaa 
= with wide eyes; stitaH = stood; upari = on the top; la~NkayaaH = of Lanka.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter that best elephant ascended by those princes Rama and Lakshmana and by Seetha with 
wide eyes stood on the top of Lanka."

verses.sanskrit:
पाण्डुरर्षभयुक्तेन रथेनाष्टयुजा स्वयम् || ५-२७-१७
इहोपयातः काकुथ्सः सीतया सह भार्य या |
verses.translation: "Rama together with wife Seetha, Himself came here in a chariot tied with eight white bulls."

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया सह वीर्यवान् || ५-२७-१८
आरुह्य पुष्पकं दिव्यं विमानं सूर्यसन्निभम् 
|
उत्तरां दिशमालोक्य जगाम पुरुष्त्तमः || ५-२७-१९
verses.translation:
"The valorous best among men Rama together with brother Lakshmana and with Seetha ascended wonderful 
Pushpaka aeroplane equalling the Sun, went in the northern direction."

verses.sanskrit:
एवं स्वप्ने मया दृष्टो रामो विष्णुपराक्रमः |
लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया सह राघवः || ५-२७-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. raamaH = Rama; viShNuparaakramaH = with strength equalling that of 
Lord Vishnu; raaghavaH = born in Raghu's dynasty; saha = together; bhraatraa lakshmaNena 
= with brother Lakshmana; siitayaa = (and) with Seetha; dR^iShTaH = has been seen; mayaa 
= by me; evam = thus; svapne = in the dream.
verses.translation:
"Rama with strength equalling that of Lord Vishnu, born in Raghu's dynasty together with brother 
Lakshmana and with Seetha has been seen by me thus in the dream."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि रामो महातेजाः शोक्यो जेतुं सुरासुरैः |
राक्षसैर्वापि चान्यैर्वा स्वर्गः पापजनैरिव 
|| ५-२७-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. raamaH = Rama; mahaatejaaH = with great glory; na shakyaH 
= is not possible; jetum = to be defeated; suraiH = by Suras; raakshasairvaapi = or 
by ogres; anvairvaa = or by others; svargaH iva = like heaven; paapajanaiH = by sinners.
verses.translation:
"Rama with great glory is not possible to be defeated by Suras or by ogres or by others like heaven 
not being conquerable by sinners."

verses.sanskrit:
रावणश्च मया दृष्टः क्षितौ तैलसमुक्षितः |
रक्तवासाह् पिबन् मत्तः करवीरकृतस्रजः|| ५-२७-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. raavanashcha = Ravana also; dR^iShTaH = was seen; mayaa = 
by me; kshitau = on earth; tailasamukshitaH = sprinkled with oil; rakshavaasaaH = with 
red clothes; karaviira kR^ita srajaH = with garlands tied with Oleander flowers; piban = drinking 
oil; mattaH = intoxicated;
verses.translation:
"Ravana also was seen by me on earth sprinkled with oil, with red clothes, with garlands tied with 
Oleander flowers, drinking oil and intoxicated."

verses.sanskrit:
विमानात्पुष्पकादद्य रावणः पतितो भुवि |
कृष्यमाणः स्त्रिया दृष्टो मुण्डः कृष्णाम्बरः पुनः 
|| ५-२७-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. adya = now; raavaNa = Ravana; patitaH = fallen down; bhuvi 
= (on to) earth; puShpakaat vimaanaat = from Pushpaka aeroplane; muNDaH = having the head shaved;
kR^iShNaambaraH = wearing black clothes; punaH = again; dR^iShTaH = was seen; kR^iShyamaaNaH 
= (to be) pulled; striyaa = by a woman.
verses.translation:
"Now Ravana fallen down on to earth from Pushpaka aeroplane having the head shaved, wearing black 
clothes again was seen to be pulled by a woman."

verses.sanskrit:
रथेन खरयुक्तेन रक्तमाल्यानुलेपनः |
पिबंस्तैलं हसन्नृतन् भ्रान्तचित्ताकुलेन्द्रियः || ५-२७-२४
verses.translation:
"Wearing red garlands and unguents, drinking oil, laughing, dancing, with a confused mind, with 
agitated senses, Ravana went by a chariot tied by donkeys."

verses.sanskrit:
गर्दभेन ययौ शीग्रं दक्षिणां दिशमास्थितः |
पुनरेव मया दृष्टो रावणो राक्षसेश्वरः || ५-२७-२५

पतितोऽवाक्छिरा भूमौ गर्दभाद्भयमोहितः |
verses.wordMeaning:
25. aasthitaH = undertaking; dakshinaam disham = southern direction;
yayau = (Ravana) went; shiighram = quickly; gardhabena = by a donkey; punareva 
= again; ma dR^iShTaH = it was seen by me; raakshasesvaraH = (that) the king of ogres; raavaNaH 
= Ravana; bhaya mohitaH = stupefied with fear; patitaH = fell down; gardhabaat = from 
the donkey; avaakchiraaH = with head down.
verses.translation:
"Undertaking southern direction, Ravana went quickly by a donkey.Again it was seen by me that the 
king of ogres Ravana stupefied with fear, fell down from the donkey with head down."

verses.sanskrit:
सहसोत्थाय संभ्रान्तो भयार्तो मदविह्वलः || ५-२७-२६
उन्मत्त इव दिग्वासा दुर्वाक्यं प्रलपन् 
बहु |
दुर्गन्धं दुस्सहं घोरं तिमिरं नरकोपमम् || ५-२७-२७
मलपङ्कं प्रविश्याशु मग्नस्तत्र स रावणः |
verses.translation:
"That Ravana rising up quickly, together with perplexity, distressed with fear, excited by passion, 
being naked, like a mad man, prattling many bad words, entering dirt with a bad smell, which was intolerable, 
horrible, dark, like the hell and quickly sank in that."

verses.sanskrit:
कण्ठे बद्ध्वा दशग्रीवं प्रमदा रक्तवासिनी || ५-२७-२८
काळी कर्दमलिप्ताङ्गी दिशं याम्यां प्रकर्षति 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
28. pramadaa = a woman; raktavaasinii = with red clothes; kaLii 
= who was black; kardamaliptaa~Ngii = with body smeared with dirt; badhvaa = tying; dashagriivam 
= Ravana; kaNThe = by the throat; prakarShati = was pulling him; yaamyaam disham = 
in southern direction.
verses.translation:
"A woman with red clothes, who was black, with body smeared with dirt, tying Ravana by the throat, 
was pulling hiim in southern direction."

verses.sanskrit:
एवं तत्र मया दृष्टह् कुम्भकर्णो निशाचरः || ५-२७-२९
रावणस्य सुताः सर्वे दृष्टास्तैलसमुक्षिताः 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
29. tatra = in that (dream); nishaacharaH = the ogre; kumbhakarNaH 
= Kumbhakarna; dR^iShTaH = has been seen; evam = thus; mayaa = by me; sarve 
= all; raavaNasya = Ravana's; sutaaH = sons; dR^iShTaaH = have been seen; tailasamukshitaaH 
= to be sprinkled with oil.
verses.translation:
"In that dream the ogre Kumbhakarna has been seen thus by me. All Ravana's sons have been seen 
to be sprinkled with oil."

verses.sanskrit:
वराहेण दशग्रीवः शिंशुमारेण चेन्द्रजित् || ५-२७-३०
उष्ट्रेण कुम्भकर्णश्च प्रयाता दक्षिणां 
दिशम् |
verses.translation: "Ravana by a pig, Indrajit by a porpoise, Kumbhakarna by a camel set out in southern direction."

verses.sanskrit:
एकस्तत्र मया दृष्टः श्वेतच्छत्रो विभीसणः || ५-२७-३१
शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरः शुक्लगन्धानुलेपनः 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
31. tatra = among those; vibhiiShaNaH = Vibhishana; ekaH = is 
the only one; dR^iShTaH = (who) has been seen; mayaa = by me; shvetachchhatraH = to 
be having a white umbrella; shuklamaalyaambaradharaH = wearing white garlands and clothes; shuklagandhaanulepanaH 
= anointing body with white unguents.
verses.translation:
"Among those Vibhishana is the only one who has been seen by me to be having a white umbrella, 
wearing white garlands and clothes and anointing body with white unguents."

verses.sanskrit:
शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषैर्नृत्तगीतैरलङ्कृतः || ५-२७-३२
आरुह्य शैलसंकाशं मेघस्तनितनिस्वनम् |

चतुर्धन्तं गजं दिव्यमास्ते तत्र विभीषणः || ५-२७-३३
चतुर्भिः सचिवैः सार्धं वहायसमुपस्थितः |
verses.translation:
"Vibhishana is there adorned with the sound of conch shells and kettledrums, with dances and songs, 
ascended a best elephant equalling a mountain with the sound of a thundering cloud, with four tusks. He approached 
the sky together with four ministers"

verses.sanskrit:
समाजश्च मया दृष्टो गीतवादित्रनिःस्वनः || ५-२७-३४
पिबतां रक्तमाल्यानां रक्षसां रक्तवाससाम् 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
34. samaajashcha = an assembly also; rakshasaam = of ogres; pibataam 
= drinking (oil); raktamaalyaanaam = wearing red garlands; raktvaasasaam = with red clothes;giitavaaditraniHsvanaH 
= with the sound of songs and musical instruments; dR^iShtaH = has been seen; mayaa = by me.
verses.translation:
"An assembly also of ogres drinking oil and wearing red garlands, with red clothes, with the sound 
of songs and musical instruments has been seen by me."

verses.sanskrit:
लङ्का चेयं पुरी रम्या सवाजिरथकुञ्जरा || ५-२७-३५
सागरे पतिता दृष्टा भग्नगोपुरतोरणा |
verses.wordMeaning:
35. iyam = this; ramyaa = beautiful; la~Nkaa purii cha = city 
of Lanka also; dR^iShTaa = has been seen; savaajirathaku~njaraaH = together with horses and 
elephants; bhagnagopuratoraNaa = with broken gates and arches; patitaa = fallen down; saagare 
= in the ocean.
verses.translation:
"This beautiful city of Lanka has also been seen together with horses and elephants with broken 
gates and arches fallen down in the ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
लङ्का दृष्टा मया स्वप्ने रावणेनाभिरक्षिता || ५-२७-३६
दग्धा रामस्य दूतेन वानरेण तरस्विना 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
36. la~Nkaa = Lanka; abhirakshitaa = protected; raavaNena = by 
Ravana; dR%iShTaa = has been seen; mayaa = by me; svapne = in the dream; dagdhaa 
= to be burnt; vaanreNa = by a Vanara; raamsya duutena = (who is) a messenger of Rama; tarasvinaa 
= (who is) a quick one;
verses.translation:
"Lanka protected by Ravana has been seen by me in the dream to be burnt by a quick Vanara who is 
a messenger of Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
सीत्वा तैलं प्रनृत्ताश्च प्रहसन्त्यो महास्वनाः || ५-२७-३७
लङ्कायाण् भस्मरूक्षायां सर्वा 
राक्षसयोषितः |
verses.wordMeaning:
37. la~Nkaayaam = in Lanka; bhasmaruukshaayaam = horrible with ash; 
sarvaaH = all; raakshasa striyaH = ogresses; piitvaa = (were) drinking; tailam 
= oil; prahasantyaH = laughing heartily; mahaasvanaaH = with a great noise; pranR^ittaaH 
cha = and also dancing a lot.
verses.translation:
"In Lanka horrible with ash, all ogresses were drinking oil, laughing heartily with a great noise 
and also dancing a lot."

verses.sanskrit:
कुम्भकर्णादयश्चेमे सर्वे राक्षसपुङ्गवाः || ५-२७-३८
रक्तं निवसनं गृह्य प्रविष्टा गोमयह्रदे 
|
verses.translation:
"All these best among ogres such as Kumbhakarna and others taking a red cloth entered a hole filled 
with cow dung."

verses.sanskrit:
अपगच्छत नश्यध्वं सीतामाप्नोति राघवः || ५-२७-३९
घातयेत्परमामर्षी युष्मान् सार्धं हि राक्षनैः 
|
verses.translation:
"Go away! Be destroyed! Rama will get Seetha. With a great anger He will get you killed together 
with other ogres."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रियां बहुमतां भार्यां वनवासमनुव्रताम् || ५-२७-४०
भर्त्सितां तर्जितां वापि नानुमंस्यति 
राघवः |
verses.wordMeaning:
40. raaghavaH = Rama; na anumaMsyati = will not approve; bharsitaam 
= frightening; tarjitaaM vaapi = or threatening; priyaam bhaaryaam = His dear wife; bahumataam 
= who is highly valued (by Him); anuvrataam = who followed Him; vanavaasam = to live in forest.
verses.translation:
"Rama will not approve frightening ot threatening His dear wife who is highly valued by Him, who 
followed Him to live in the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
तदलं क्रूरवाक्यैश्च सान्त्वमेवाभिधीयताम् || ५-२७-४१
अभियाचाम वैदेहीमेतद्धि मम रोचते |
verses.wordMeaning:
41. tat = for that reason; alam = enough; kruura vaakyaiH = of 
cruel words; saantvameva = gentle words indeed; abhidiiyataam = be said; abhiyaachaama 
= (let us) request; vaidehiim = Seetha; etaddhi = this; rochate = is agreeable; 
me = to me.
verses.translation:
"For that reason, enough of cruel words. Gentle words indeed be said. Let us request Seetha. This 
is agreeable to me."

verses.sanskrit:
यस्यामेवंविधः स्वप्नो दुःखितायां प्रदृश्यते || ५-२७-४२
सा दुःखैर्विविधैर्मुक्ता प्रियं 
प्राप्नोत्यनुत्तमम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
42. yasyaam = which woman; duHkhitaayaam = while being sorrowful; evaMvidhaH 
= such type; svapnaH = (of) dream; pradR^ishyate = has been seen;saa = that woman; muktaa 
= being released; vividhaiH duHkhaiH = from various sorrows; praapnoti = will get; anuttamam 
= unsurpassed; priyam = pleasure.
verses.translation:
"Which woman while being sorrowful, such type of dream has been seen, that woman being released 
from various sorrows, will get unsurpassed pleasure."

verses.sanskrit:
भर्त्सितामपि याचध्वं राक्सस्यः किं विवक्षया || ५-२७-४३
राघवाद्धि भयं घोरं राक्षसानामुपस्थितम् 
|
verses.translation:
"O ogresses! Entreat Seetha who has been frightened. What is the use with a desire to speak. From 
Rama a horrible fear has come to ogres."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रणिपातप्रसन्ना हि मैथिली जनकात्मजा || ५-२७-४४
अलमेषा परित्रारुं राक्षस्यो महातो भयात् 
|
अपि चास्या विशालाक्ष्या न किंचिदुपलक्षये || ५-२७-४५
विरूपमपि चाङ्गेषु सुसूक्ष्ममपि लक्षणम् |

चायावैगुण्यमात्रं तु शङ्के दुःखमुपस्थितम् || ५-२७-४६
अदुःखार्हमिमां देवीं वैहायसमुपस्थिताम् 
|
verses.wordMeaning:
46. chaayaavaiguNya maatraM = (I see) an imperfection in complexion alone; sha~Nke 
= I doubt; imaam deviim = (that) this princess; upasthitaam = who came near; vaihaayasam 
= an aeroplane (in my dream); aduHkhaarhaam = who is not suited to sorrows; upasthitam = received;
duHkham = sorrow.
verses.translation:
"I see an imperfection in complexion alone. I doubt that this princess who came near an aeroplane 
in my dream, who is not suited to sorrows received sorrow."

verses.sanskrit:
अर्थसिद्धिं तु वैदेह्याः पश्याम्यहमुपस्थिताम् || ५-२७-४७
राक्षसेन्द्रविनाशं च विजयं राघवस्य 
च |
verses.wordMeaning:
47. aham = I; pashyaami = am seeing; ardhasiddhiM tu = accomplished 
motive; vaidehyaaH = of Seetha; upasthitaam = coming near; raakshasendra vinaasham cha 
= the destruction of Ravana also; raaghavasya vijayaM cha = and victory of Rama (coming near).
verses.translation:
"I am seeing Seetha coming near accomplishment of motive. I also see the destruction of Ravana 
also and victory of Rama coming near."

verses.sanskrit:
निमित्तभूतमेतत्तु श्रोतुमस्या महत्प्रियम् || ५-२७-४८
दृश्यते च स्फुरच्चक्षुः पद्मपत्रमिवायतम् 
|
verses.translation:
"Being a cause to listen to a great pleasure of Her, this throbbing eye long like a lotus petal 
is being seen my me."

verses.sanskrit:
ईषच्च हृषितो वास्या दक्षिणाया ह्यदक्षिणः || ५-२७-४९
अकस्मादेव वैदेह्या बहुरेकः प्रकम्पते 
|
verses.translation: "One left arm of this strightforward Seetha suddenly has become erect and is slightly trembling."

verses.sanskrit:
करेणुहस्तप्रतिमः सव्यश्चोरुरनुत्तमः || ५-२७-५०
वेपमानः सूचयति राघवं पुरतः स्थितम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
50. anuttamaH savyaH uuruH = (Seetha's) best left thigh; kareNuhastapratimaH 
= equal to an elephant's trunk; vepamaanaH = is trembling; suuchayati = indicating; raaghavam 
= Rama's; sthitam = is standing; purataH = in front (of Her).
verses.translation:
"Seetha's best left thigh equal to an elephant's trunk is trembling indicating Rama's standing 
in front of Her."

verses.sanskrit:
पक्षी च शाखानिलयं प्रविष्टः |
पुनः पुनश्चोत्तमसान्त्ववादी |
सुस्वागतं वाचमुदीरयानः |

पुनः पुनश्चोदयतीव हृष्टः || ५-२७-५१
verses.wordMeaning:
51. pakshii cha = a bird also; praviShTaH = entering; shaakhaa nilayam 
= (its) dwelling place on a branch; punaH punashcha = again and again; uttamasaantvavaadii = 
speaking best consolating words; susvaagatam vaacham udiirayaanaH = telliing heartily welcoming words;
hR^iShTaH = being delighted; punaH punaH = again and again; chodayatiiva = as though 
impelling (Seetha).
verses.translation:
"A bird also entering its dwelling place on a branch again and again speaking best consolating 
words, is telling heartily welcoming words being delighted again and again, as though impelling Seetha."
verses.commentary:
© July, 2005, Kishore Kopalle
