sargas.title: Sarga 67
sargas.introduction:
Hanuma narrates at length an incident connected with a crow that occured during the sojourn 
of Seetha with Rama at Chitrakuta, which was related by Seetha by way of a token of remembrance. Seetha 
then describes her miserable state to Hanuma and requests him to ask Rama, Lakshmana and Sugreeva to 
came to Lanka to destroy Ravana together with his army. Hanuma consoles Seetha, saying that Rama and 
Lakshmana would reduce Lanka to ashes and take her back to Ayodhya. When Hanuma further requests Seetha 
to give a token of remembrance to be handed over to Rama, she gives him a jewel for the head as a token.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तः तु हनुमान् राघवेण महात्मना |

सीताया भाषितम् सर्वम् न्यवेदयत राघवे || ५-६७-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. evam = thus; uktaH = spoken; mahaatmanaa = by the 
high-souled; raaghaveNa = Rama; hanumaan = Hanuma; nyavedayata = reported;
raaghave = to Rama; sarvam = all; bhaaSitam = the words; siitaayaaH 
= of Seetha.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तवती देवी जानकी पुरुष ऋषभ |

पूर्व वृत्तम् अभिज्ञानम् चित्र कूटे यथा तथम् || ५-६७-२
verses.translation:
"O Rama the foremost of men! Seetha, the princess, spoke a detailed account of an event 
which occurred long ago in Mount Chitrakuta, as a token of recollection."

verses.sanskrit:
सुख सुप्ता त्वया सार्धम् जानकी पूर्वम् उत्थिता |

वायसः सहसा उत्पत्य विरराद स्तन अन्तरे || ५-६७-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. jaanakii = Seetha; sukha suptaa = who slept happily; tvayaa 
saartham = with you; utthitaa = got up; puurvam = before you; vaayasaH 
= a crow; utpatya = coming down; sahasaa = quickly; vidadaara = tore the 
flesh; stanaantare = at an area in the midst of her breasts (with its beak).
verses.translation:
"Seetha, who slept happily by your side (at one instance) got up before you. A crow coming 
down quickly, tore her flesh at an area in the midst of her breasts (with its beak)."

verses.sanskrit:
पर्यायेण च सुप्तः त्वम् देवि अन्के भरत अग्रज |

पुनः च किल पक्षी स देव्या जनयति व्यथाम् || ५-६७-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. bharata agraja = O Rama!; tvam = you; suptaH = slept;
paryaayeNa = in your turn; devi anke = on the lap of the princes; saH pakSii 
= that crow; punaH ca = again; janayati kila vyathaam = began to torment; devyaaH 
= the princess.
verses.translation:
"O Rama! You slept in your turn on the lap of the princess. That crow again began to 
torment the princess."

verses.sanskrit:
पुनः पुनरुपागम्य विरराद भृशम् किल |

ततः त्वम् बोधितः तस्याः शोणितेन समुक्षितः || ५-६७-५
verses.translation:
"Coming near again and again, it tore her breast severely. Dampened by her blood, you 
then were awakened by her."

verses.sanskrit:
वायसेन च तेन एव सततम् बाध्यमानया |

बोधितः किल देव्याः त्वम् सुख सुप्तः परम् तप || ५-६७-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. param tapa = O tormentator of enemies!; devyaa = by Seetha 
the princess; baadhyamaanayaa = who was being tormented; satatam = ceaselessly;
te vaayasenaiva = by that crow; tvam = you; sukhasuptaH = who were sleeping 
happily; bodhitaH kila = were awakened.
verses.translation:
"O tormentator of enemies! Though lying happily asleep, you were awakened by Seetha the 
princess, who was being ceaselessly tormented by the crow."

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् तु दृष्ट्वा महाबाहो रादिताम् च स्तन अन्तरे |

आशी विष इव क्रुद्धो निह्श्वसन्न् अभ्यभाषथाः || ५-६७-७
verses.translation:
"O long-armed Rama! Seeing her, who was lacerated at the midst of her breasts, you were 
enraged like a hissing snake and spoke to her as follows."

verses.sanskrit:
नख अग्रैः केन ते भीरु दारितम् तु स्तन अन्तरम् |

कः क्रीडति सरोषेण पन्च वक्त्रेण भोगिना || ५-६७-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. bhiiru = O bashful one!; kena = by whom; te stana antaram 
= was your mid-area of your breasts; daaritam = lacerated; nakha agraiH = with nail-points?;
kaH kriiDati = who is playing; saroSeNa = with an angry; pancha vaktreNa 
= five-hooded; bhoginaa = serpent?
verses.translation:
" 'O bashful Seetha! By whom was the middle of your breasts lacerated with nail-points? 
Who is playing with an angry five-hooded serpent?"

verses.sanskrit:
निरीक्षमाणः सहसा वायसम् समवैक्षताः |

नखैः सरुधिरैः तीक्ष्णैः माम् एव अभिमुखम् स्थितम् || ५-६७-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. niriikSamaaNaH = looking around; samavaikShataaH = you saw;
sahasaa = suddenly; vaayasam = a crew; sthitam = which perched; taameva 
abhimukham = in front of her; tiikSNaiH nakhaiH = with its sharp nails; sarudhiraiH 
= stained with blood.
verses.translation:
"Looking around, you suddenly saw a crow, which perched in front of Seetha, with its 
sharp nails stained with blood."

verses.sanskrit:
सुतः किल स शक्रस्य वायसः पतताम् वरः |

धरा अन्तर चरः शीघ्रम् पवनस्य गतौ समः || ५-६७-१०
verses.translation:
"That crow, foremost of the birds was indeed the son of Indra the lord of celestials; 
dwelling in the middle of the mountains and equal in speed to the wind."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः तस्मिन् महाबाहो कोप सम्वर्तित ईक्षणः |

वायसे त्वम् कृत्वाः क्रूराम् मतिम् मतिमताम् वर || ५-६७-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. mahaabaaho = O great armed one!; matimataam vara = O excellent 
among the wise! Kopa samvarta iikShaNaH = with your eyes rolling with anger; tvam = you;
tataH = then; kR^ithaaH = harboured; kruuraam = a cruel; matim = 
intention; tasmin vaayase = against that crow.
verses.translation:
"O great armed Rama, the excellent among the wise! With your eyes rolling with anger, 
you then harboured a cruel intention against that crow."

verses.sanskrit:
स दर्भम् संस्तरात् गृह्य ब्रह्म अस्त्रेण न्ययोजयः |

प्रदीप्त इव काल अग्निः जज्वाल अभिमुखः खगम् || ५-६७-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. gR^ihya = taking; darbham = a blade of grass; samstaraat 
= from you bed of the sacred grass; ayojayaH = you charged it; braahma astreNa = with 
the potency of Brahma missile; saH diiptaH = that flaming blade; jajvaala = blazed 
forth; kaala agniH iva = like the fire of universal dissolution; khagam abhimukhaH 
= with its end facing that bird.
verses.translation:
"Taking a blade of grass from your bed of the sacred grass, you charged it with the potency 
of Brahma missile. That flaming blade blazed forth like the fire of universal dissolution, with its 
end facing the bird."

verses.sanskrit:
क्षिप्तवांस्त्वम् प्रदीप्तम् दर्भम् तम् वायसम् प्रति |

ततः तु वायसम् दीप्तः स दर्भो अनुजगाम ह || ५-६७-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. tvam = you; kSiptavaan = hurled; tam pradiiptam darbham 
= that flaming blade of grass; vaayasam prati = towards the crow; tataH = then;
saH diiptaH darbhaH = that flaming blade of grass; anujagaama = followed; vaayasam 
= the crow.
verses.translation:
"You hurled that flaming blade of grass towards the crow. Then that flaming blade of 
grass for its part followed the crow."

verses.sanskrit:
स पित्रा च परित्यक्तः सुरैः सर्वैः महर्षिभिः |

त्रीन् लोकान् सम्परिक्रम्य त्रातारम् न अधिगच्छति || ५-६७-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. partiyaktaH = forsaken; suraisH ca = by all the celestials;
pitraa ca = its father; Indra; sa maharSibhiH = and the sages also; saH = 
(that crows) na adhigacchati = did not find; traataaram = a protector; samparikramya 
= after going round; triin lokaan = the three worlds (heave; earth and the lower regions).
verses.translation:
"Forsaken by all the celestials, Indra, its father and the sages too, that crow did not 
find a protector even after going round all the three worlds (heaven, earth and the lower regions)."

verses.sanskrit:
पुनरेवागतस्त्रस्तस्त्वत्सकाशमरिंदम |

स तम् निपतितम् भूमौ शरण्यः शरणा गतम् || ५-६७-१५

वध अर्हम् अपि काकुत्स्थ कृपया परिपालयः |
verses.translation:
"O destroyer of enemies! Being frightened the crow came to your presence once again and 
sough your protection. Though fit to be killed, you on your part, affording shelter, in mercy to the 
crow, protected that crow which had fallen flat on the ground."

verses.sanskrit:
मोघम् अस्त्रम् न शक्यम् तु कर्तुम् इति एव राघव || ५-६७-१६

भवांस्तस्यक्षि काकस्य हिनस्ति स्म स दक्षिणम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
16. raaghava = O Rama!; ityeva = because; na shakyam 
= it is not possible; kartum = to make; astram = the missile; mogham = a 
waste; bhavaan = you; hinanti sma = destroyed; dakSinam akSi = the right 
eye; kaakasya = of the cow.

verses.sanskrit:
राम त्वाम् स नमः कृत्वा राज्ञो दशरथस्य च || ५-६७-१७

विसृष्टः तु तदा काकः प्रतिपेदे खम् आलयम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
14. partiyaktaH = forsaken; suraisH ca = by all the celestials;
pitraa ca = its father; Indra; sa maharSibhiH = and the sages also; saH = 
(that crows) na adhigacchati = did not find; traataaram = a protector; samparikramya 
= after going round; triin lokaan = the three worlds (heave; earth and the lower regions).
verses.translation:
"O Rama! Then, that crow, having been discharged by you, went back to its own habitation, 
after offering salutation to you and to king Dasaratha."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् अस्त्रविदाम् श्रेष्ठः सत्त्ववान् शीलवान् अपि || ५-६७-१८

किम् अर्थम् अस्त्रम् रक्षह्सु न योजयसि राघव |
verses.wordMeaning:
18. raaghava = O Rama!; kim artham = why; na yojayasi 
= are you not employing; astram = the missile; evam = thus; rakShassu = on 
the demons; shreSThaH api = though foremost; astravidaam = among the wielders courageous;
shiilavaan = and possessed of good disposition.
verses.translation:
"O Rama! Why are you not employing the same type of a missile on the demons, though you 
are the foremost among the wielders of missiles, though courageous and possessed of good disposition?"

verses.sanskrit:
न नागा न अपि गन्धर्वा न असुरा न मरुत् गणाः || ५-६७-१९

न च सर्वे रणे शक्ता रामम् प्रतिसमासितुम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
19. naagaaH = Nagas the celestials serpents; na shaktaaH = are 
not capable; na gandharvaaH api = Nor even Gandharvas the celestial musicians; na marut 
gaNaaH = nor the troops of maruts; the na sarve = nor all of them put together; pratisamaasitum 
= of fighting; raamam prati = against Rama; raNe = in battle.
verses.translation:
"Neither the Nagas the celestials serpents nor even Gandharvas the celestial musicians 
nor the demons nor the troop of Maruts the host of gods, nor all of them joined together are capable 
of fighting with Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य वीर्यवतः कच्चिन् मयि यदि अस्ति सम्भ्रमः || ५-६७-२०

क्षिप्रम् सुनिशितैः बाणैः हन्यताम् युधि रावणः |
verses.wordMeaning:
20. yadi = If; viiryavataH tasya = to that powerful Rama; 
sambhramaH asti = there is eagerness; mayi = in my matter; hanyataam raavaNaH 
= let Ravana; be killed; yudhi = in battle; kShipram = quickly; sunishitaiH baaNaiH 
= with very sharp arrows.
verses.translation:
"If that powerful Rama is eager to save me, let him kill Ravana in battle quickly with 
his exceptionally sharp arrows."

verses.sanskrit:
भ्रातुः आदेशम् आदाय लक्ष्मणो वा परम् तपः || ५-६७-२१

स किम् अर्थम् नर वरो न माम् रक्षति राघवः |
verses.translation:
"Why even that Lakshmana, the annihilator of adversaries and the foremost of men born 
in Raghu dynasty is not saving me, by taking orders from Rama his brother?"

verses.sanskrit:
शक्तौ तौ पुरुष व्याघ्रौ वाय्व् अग्नि सम तेजसौ || ५-६७-२२

सुराणाम् अपि दुर्धर्षौ किम् अर्थम् माम् उपेक्षतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. kim artham = wherefore; tau shaktau = those two competent 
brothers; Rama and Lakshmana; vaayv agni sama tejasau = having their splendour similar to that 
of wind and fire; puruSa vyaaghrau = and the excellent among men; durdharSau.api = 
even though they can conquer; suraaNaam = the celestials; upekSataH = are ignoring;
maam = me?
verses.translation:
"Wherefore those two competent brothers Rama and Lakshmana, with their splendour equal 
to that of air and fire, and excellent among men, even though they can conquer the celestials, are ignoring 
me?"

verses.sanskrit:
मम एव दुष्कृतम् किंचिन् महत् अस्ति न संशयः || ५-६७-२३

समर्थौ सहितौ यन् माम् न अपेक्षेते परम् तपौ |
verses.wordMeaning:
23. kimchit = some; mahat = great; duSkR^itam = impure 
act; asti = was there; mamaiva = on my part; na samshayaH = there is no doubt;
yat = for which reason; tau = those two brothers; Rama and Lakshmana; param tapau 
= who torment the enemies; samarthaavapi = eventhough capable; na avekSate = are not 
paying attention; maam = to me.
verses.translation:
"There is no doubt that I had done some big impure act, for which reason those two brothers 
Rama and Lakshmana, who torment the enemies, even though capable, are not paying attention to me."

verses.sanskrit:
वैदेह्या वचनम् श्रुत्वा करुणम् साश्रु भाषितम् || ५-६७-२४

पुनः अपि अहम् आर्याम् ताम् इदम् वचनम् अब्रुवम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
24. shrutvaa = hearing; karuNam = the miserable; vachanam 
= words; vaidehyaaH = of Seetha; saashru bhaaSitam = spoken with tears; aham 
= I; punaH api = again; abruvam = spoke; taam aaryaam = to that venerable 
Seetha; idam vachanam = the following words:

verses.sanskrit:
त्वत् शोक विमुखो रामो देवि सत्येन ते शपे || ५-६७-२५

रामे दुह्ख अभिभूते च लक्ष्मणः परितप्यते |
verses.wordMeaning:
25. devi = O Seetha the princess!; raamaH = Rama; tvat shoka 
vimukhaH = has grown indifferent because of grief born of separation from you; shape = 
I swear; te = to you; satyena = truly; raame = Rama; duhkha abhibhuute 
= being overcome with grief; lakSmaNaH cha = Lakshmana too; paritapyate = is feeling 
pain.
verses.translation:
" 'O Seetha the princess! Rama has grown indifferent because of grief born of separation 
from you. I swear it to you truly. Rama, being overwhelmed with grief, Lakshmana too is feeling pain."

verses.sanskrit:
कथंचित् भवती दृष्टा न कालः परिशोचितुम् || ५-६७-२६

अस्मिन्मुहूर्तम् दुह्खानाम् अन्तम् द्रक्ष्यसि भामिनि |
verses.wordMeaning:
26. anindite = O faultless one!; bhavatii = you; dR^iSTaa 
= have been seen; kathamchit = somehow; na kaalam = this is not time; devitum 
= to lament; drakSyasi = you will see; antam = the end; duhkhaanaam = of 
your sorrows; imam muhuurtam = during this very moment.
verses.translation:
" 'O faultless one! You can give now, a token of recollection, which Rama can recognize 
and which can beget happiness to him."

verses.sanskrit:
ताव् उभौ नर शार्दूलौ राज पुत्राव् अरिम् दमौ || ५-६७-२७

त्वत् दर्शन कृत उत्साहौ लन्काम् भस्मी करिष्यतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
27. ubhau tau raja putrau = those two princes; nara shaarduulau 
= the foremost among men; aninditau = and the faultless ones; tavat darshana kR^ita utsaahau 
= who are eager to see you; laN^kaam bhasmii kariSyataH = can reduce Lanka to ashes.
verses.translation:
" 'Those two princes, the foremost among men and the faultless ones are eager to see 
you. They can reduce Lanka to ashes'."

verses.sanskrit:
हत्वा च समरे रौद्रम् रावणम् सह बान्धवम् || ५-६७-२८

राघवः त्वाम् महाबाहुः स्वाम् पुरीम् नयते ध्रुवम् |
verses.translation:
" 'O lady with charming hips! Killing the terrific Ravana together with his relatives 
in battle, Rama will certainly take you to his City of Ayodhya'."

verses.sanskrit:
यत् तु रामो विजानीयात् अभिज्ञानम् अनिन्दिते || ५-६७-२९

प्रीति सम्जननम् तस्य प्रदातुम् तत्त्वम् अर्हसि |
verses.wordMeaning:
29. anindite = O faultless one!; tvam = you; arhasi 
= ought; daatum = to give; iha = now; abhiJNaanam = a token of recollection;
yat = which; raamaH = Rama; vijaaniiyaat = can recognize; priiti samjananam 
= and which can beget happiness; tasya = to him.
verses.translation:
" 'O faultless one! You can give now, a token of recollection, which Rama can recognize 
and which can beget happiness to him."

verses.sanskrit:
सा अभिवीक्ष्य दिशः सर्वा वेणि उद्ग्रथनम् उत्तमम् || ५-६७-३०

मुक्त्वा वस्त्रात् ददौ मह्यम् मणिम् एतम् महाबल |
verses.translation:
"O mighty Rama! Having surveyed all the directions and having untied from the end of 
her garment, this excellent jewel which was being strung together in her hair that Seetha gave it to 
me."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रतिगृह्य मणिम् दिव्यम् तव हेतो रघु उत्तम || ५-६७-३१

शिरसा सम्प्रणम्य एनाम् अहम् आगमने त्वरे |
verses.wordMeaning:
31. raghuudvaha = O Rama!; pratigR^ihya = taking; divyam 
maNim = that wonderful jewel; tava hetoH = for your sake; praNamya = and offering 
my salutation; shirasaa = by bowing my head; aaryaam taam = to that venerable Seetha;
aham = I; tvare = was seized with a haste; aagamane = to come back.
verses.translation:
"O Rama! Taking that wonderful jewel for your sake from her, and offering my salutation 
by bowing my head to that venerable Seetha, I was seized with a haste to come back."

verses.sanskrit:
गमने च कृत उत्साहम् अवेक्ष्य वर वर्णिनी || ५-६७-३२

विवर्धमानम् च हि माम् उवाच जनक आत्मजा |
verses.wordMeaning:
32. avekSya = seeing; maam = me; kR^ita utsaaham = who 
was in a hurry; gamane = to come back; vivardhamaanam cha = and augmenting my body 
abundantly; jaanaka atmajaa = Seetha; varavarNinii = having a beautiful complexion;
uvaacha = spoke.
verses.translation:
"Seeing me who was in a hurry to come back and augmenting my body abundantly, Seetha 
having a beautiful complexion spoke to me."

verses.sanskrit:
अश्रु पूर्ण मुखी दीना बाष्प संदिग्ध भाषिणी || ५-६७-३३

ममोत्पतनसम्ब्रान्ता शोकवेगसमाहता |
verses.wordMeaning:
33. ashru puurNa mukhii = with her eyes filled with tears; diinaa 
= looking miserable; baSpa samdigdha bhaaSiNii = with ambiguous words coming out of her choked 
throat; mama utpatana sambhraantaa = bewildered as she was that I was going to fly away; (she 
spoke to me as follows); shokavega samaahataa = afflicted with a gush a grief.
verses.translation:
"With her eyes filled with tears, looking miserable, with her words ambiguously coming 
out of her coked throat, bewildered as she was, at the though of my flying away and afflicted with a 
gush of grief; (she spoke to me as follows):

verses.sanskrit:
हनुमन् सिम्ह सम्काशौ ताव् उभौ राम लक्ष्मणौ || ५-६७-३४

सुग्रीवम् च सह अमात्यम् सर्वान् ब्रूया अनामयम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
34. hanumaan = O Hanuma!; bruuyaaH = ask; tau ubhau raama 
lakSmaNau = those two brothers; Rama and Lakshmana; sugriivam cha = Sugreeva; saha 
amaatyam = together with his ministers; sarvaan = and all others; anaamayam = 
about their well-being.
verses.translation:
"O Hanuma! Inquire those two brothers Rama and Lakshmana looking like lions, Sugreeva 
together with his ministers and all others about their welfare."

verses.sanskrit:
यथा च स महाबाहुः माम् तारयति राघवः |

अस्मात् दुह्ख अम्बु सम्रोधात् तत् समाधातुम् अर्हसि || ५-६७-३५

इमम् च तीव्रम् मम शोक वेगम् |

रक्षोभिः एभिः परिभर्त्सनम् च |

ब्रूयाः तु रामस्य गतः समीपम् |

शिवः च ते अध्वा अस्तु हरि प्रवीर || ५-६७-३६

एतत् तव आर्या नृप राज सिम्ह |

सीता वचः प्राह विषाद पूर्वम् |

एतच् च बुद्ध्वा गदितम् मया त्वम् |

श्रद्धत्स्व सीताम् कुशलाम् समग्राम् || ५-६७-३७
verses.translation:
" 'You ought to clear up difficulties for that mighty armed Rama, so that he can help 
me to cross this ocean of sorrow. O best of heroes among monkeys! Approaching Rama, tell this horrible 
gush of my grief and the threats of these female-demons. Have a happy journey!' "O illustrious princes, 
the protector of men! The venerable Seetha filled with sorrow spoke these words to be conveyed to you. 
Comprehending this matter spoken by me, believe that Seetha is completely safe."
verses.commentary:

sargas.title: Sarga 68
sargas.introduction:
Hanuma informs Rama how Seetha expressed her grave doubt as to how monkeys and bears 
can leap across a vast sea, as well as her appeal to Rama to destroy Ravana along with his entire army 
in Lanka and take her back to Ayodhya. Hanuma further informs Rama, how he resolved her doubt by explaining 
the prowess of the troops of monkeys and bears, commanded by Sugreeva as also how he brought solace 
and peace to her from her worries.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ अहम् उत्तरम् देव्या पुनः उक्तः ससम्भ्रमम् |

तव स्नेहान् नर व्याघ्र सौहार्यात् अनुमान्य च || ५-६८-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. naravyaaghra = O the excellent among men!; atha = thereafter;
anumaanya = having shown respect for me; tava snehaat = out of affection; sauhaardraat 
= and good-heartedness; tava = for you; aham = I; sasambhramam = who was 
in a haste (to go); uktaH = was spoken to; punaH = again; devyaa = by Seetha 
the princess; uttaram = about the future.
verses.translation:
"O excellent among men! Having shown respect for me out of her affection and good-heartedness 
for you, I, who was in a haste to go, was spoken to again by Seetha the princess about the further course 
of action."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् बहु विधम् वाच्यो रामो दाशरथिः त्वया |

यथा माम् आप्नुयात् शीघ्रम् हत्वा रावणम् आहवे || ५-६८-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. raamaH = Rama; daasharathiH = the son of Dasaratha; vaachyaH 
= is to be informed; evam = thus; tvayaa = by you; bahuvidham = in several 
ways; shiighram hatvaa = for quickly killing; raavaNam = Ravana; aahave = 
in battle; yathaa tathaa = and as to how; maam aapnuyaat = he can get me back.
verses.translation:
"Rama, the son of Dasaratha, is to be informed by you thus in several ways, for quickly 
killing Ravana in battle and also as to how he can get me back."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि वा मन्यसे वीर वस एका अहम् अरिम् दम |

कस्मिंश्चित् सम्वृते देशे विश्रान्तः श्वो गमिष्यसि || ५-६८-३
verses.translation:
" 'O hero the annihilator of enemies! If you deem it fit, stay for a day in a secret 
place. You can go tomorrow after taking rest'."

verses.sanskrit:
मम च अपि अल्प भाग्यायाः साम्निध्यात् तव वानर |

अस्य शोक विपाकस्य मुहूर्तम् स्यात् विमोक्षणम् || ५-६८-४
verses.translation:
"'O the valiant one! Because of your presence, the termination of my full-blown grief 
may continue for a while'."

verses.sanskrit:
गते हि त्वयि विक्रान्ते पुनः आगमनाय वै |

प्राणानाम् अपि संदेहो मम स्यान् न अत्र संशयः || ५-६८-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. tvayi = (when) you; vikraante = the courageous one; gate 
= go; punaraagamanaaya = for coming back; syaat = there is; sandehaH = an 
uncertainity; mama praaNaamapi = even to my life; na sandehaH = there is no doubt;
atra = about this.
verses.translation:
"'When you, the courageous one, have actually left to come back, there will be a danger 
even to my life. There is no doubt about this'."

verses.sanskrit:
तव अदर्शनजः शोको भूयो माम् परितापयेत् |

दुह्खात् दुह्ख पराभूताम् दुर्गताम् दुह्ख भागिनीम् || ५-६८-६
verses.translation:
"'The sorrow born of your disappearance will further torment me, who is overtaken by 
one sorrow after another, miserable and doomed to grief as I am'."

verses.sanskrit:
अयम् तु वीर संदेहः तिष्ठति इव मम अग्रतः |

सुमहान् त्वत् सहायेषु हरि ऋक्षेषु असंशयः || ५-६८-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. hariishvara = O best among monkeys; viira = the valiant one! 
haryR^ikSeSu = in the matter of monkeys and bears; tvatsahayeSu = who are helpmates to you;
ayam = this; sumahaan = a very grave; sandeshaH = doubt; tiSThatiiva 
= stands; agrataH = before me.
verses.translation:
"'O best among the monkeys! O valiant one! In the matter of monkeys and bears who are 
your helpmates, this very grave doubt stands before me'."

verses.sanskrit:
कथम् नु खलु दुष्पारम् तरिष्यन्ति महाउदधिम् |

तानि हरि ऋक्ष सैन्यानि तौ वा नर वर आत्मजौ || ५-६८-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kathamnu = how; khalu = indeed; taani = those;
haryR^ikSasainyaani = troops of monkeys and bears; tau naravaraatmajau vaa = or those 
two princes; tariSyanti = can cross; duSpaaram = the insurmountable; mahodadhim 
= great ocean?
verses.translation:
"'How indeed can those troops of monkeys and bears or Rama and Lakshmana can cross that 
great insurmountable ocean?'"

verses.sanskrit:
त्रयाणाम् एव भूतानाम् सागरस्य अस्य लन्घने |

शक्तिः स्यात् वैनतेयस्य वायोः वा तव वा अनघ || ५-६८-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. trayaaNaam = only three; bhuutaanaam = beings; vainateyasyavaa 
= viz. Garuda the eagle; tava vaa = or yourself; maarutasyavaa = or the wind-god;
syaat = have; shaktiH = the capacity; laN^ghane = in crossing; asya 
= of this; saagarasya = ocean.
verses.translation:
"'Three beings viz. Garuda the eagle, or yourself or the wind-god only have the capacity 
of crossing this ocean'."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अस्मिन् कार्य नियोगे वीर एवम् दुरतिक्रमे |

किम् पश्यसि समाधानम् ब्रूहि कार्यविदाम् वर || ५-६८-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. viira = O valiant monkey!; tat = that is why; kim 
= what; samaadhaanam = strategy; pashyasi = are you visualizing; asmin kaarya 
niryoga = to fulfill this act; duratikrame = which is so difficult to accomplish; 
evam = thus?; tvam = you; varaH his = are indeed excellent; kaaryavidaam 
= of those who know how to accomplish any work.
verses.translation:
"'O valiant monkey! That is why, what strategy are you visualizing to fulfill this act, 
which is so difficult to accomplish thus? You are indeed excellent of those who know how to accomplish 
any work'."

verses.sanskrit:
कामम् अस्य त्वम् एव एकः कार्यस्य परिसाधने ||

पर्याप्तः पर वीरघ्न यशस्यः ते बल उदयः || ५-६८-११
verses.translation:
"O Hanuma the annihilator of hostile enemies! You, as one alone, are capable of fulfilling 
this task. Your act of fulfilling this task your act of fulfilling the result is well-known."

verses.sanskrit:
बलैः समग्रैः यदि माम् हत्वा रावणम् आहवे |

विजयी स्वाम् पुरीम् रामो नयेत् तत् स्यात् यशः करम् || ५-६८-१२
verses.translation:
""It would result in his glory, if Rama, after killing Ravana along with his entire army, 
takes me to his city in his victory'."

verses.sanskrit:
यथा अहम् तस्य वीरस्य वनात् उपधिना हृता |

रक्षसा तत् भयात् एव तथा न अर्हति राघवः || ५-६८-१३
verses.translation:
"'Rama ought not to take me back for fear that Ravana, in the same manner as Ravana the 
demon took me away from the forest in the absence of Rama by cheating that heroic Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
बलैः तु सम्कुलाम् कृत्वा लन्काम् पर बल अर्दनः |

माम् नयेत् यदि काकुत्स्थः तत् तस्य सदृशम् भवेत् || ५-६८-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. yadi kaakutsthsaH = If Rama; para bala ardanaH = the destroyer 
of army of adversaries; kR^itvaa = making; laN^kaam = Lanka; samkulaam = 
filled; sharaiH = with arrows; maam nayet = takes me home; tat = that;
sadR^isham bhavet = will be worthy; tasya = of him.

verses.sanskrit:
तत् यथा तस्य विक्रान्तम् अनुरूपम् महात्मनः |

भवति आहव शूरस्य तथा त्वम् उपपादय || ५-६८-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. tat = for that reason; tvam = you; upapaadaya = 
act; yathaa = in such a way; tathaa = that; mahaatmanaH = the high-souled 
Rama; aahava shuurasya = valiant in battle; bhavet = may exhibit; vikraantam 
= the prowess; anurumpam = worthy; tasya = of him.
verses.translation:
"For that reason, you act in such a way, that the high-souled Rama, valiant in battle, 
exhibits the prowess befitting of him."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अर्थ उपहितम् वाक्यम् प्रश्रितम् हेतु सम्हितम् |

निशम्य अहम् ततः शेषम् वाक्यम् उत्तरम् अब्रुवम् || ५-६८-१६
verses.translation:
"Hearing that appeal (of Seetha), that was somewhat good in its meaning, courteous and 
duly furnished with reasons, I gave then my remaining reply (as follows):

verses.sanskrit:
देवि हरि ऋक्ष सैन्यानाम् ईश्वरः प्लवताम् वरः |

सुग्रीवः सत्त्व सम्पन्नः तव अर्थे कृत निश्चयः || ५-६८-१७
verses.translation:
"'O Seetha the princess! Sugreeva, the lord of the troops of monkeys and bears, excellent 
among the monkeys and endowed with strength, is firmly determined to rescue you."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य विक्रम सम्पन्नाः सत्त्ववन्तो महाबलाः |

मनः सम्कल्प सम्पाता निदेशे हरयः स्थिताः || ५-६८-१८

येषाम् न उपरि न अधस्तान् न तिर्यक् सज्जते गतिः |

न च कर्मसु सीदन्ति महत्स्व् अमित तेजसः || ५-६८-१९
verses.translation:
"So many monkeys, endowed with prowess, perseverance great strength and who can rush 
to any place as they wish, are intensely committed to Sugreeva's command. There is no hindrance to their 
movement either in the sky or the ground or in a horizontal region between the sky and the ground. Those 
monkeys with unlimited splendour are not indolent in performing any great task."

verses.sanskrit:
असकृत् तैः महाभागैः वानरैः बल सम्युतैः |

प्रदक्षिणी कृता भूमिः वायु मार्ग अनुसारिभिः || ५-६८-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. taiH mahaabhagaiH vaanaraiH = by those illustrious monkeys; bala 
darpitaiH = proud of their strength; vaayumaargaanusaaribhiH = and who follow the path 
of wind; bhuumiH = the earth; pradakSiNii kR^itaa = was circumambulated.
verses.translation:
"Those illustrious monkeys, proud of their strength and following the path of wind, circumambulated 
the entire earth several times."

verses.sanskrit:
मत् विशिष्टाः च तुल्याः च सन्ति तत्र वन ओकसः |

मत्तः प्रत्यवरः कश्चिन् न अस्ति सुग्रीव सम्निधौ || ५-६८-२१

अहम् तावत् इह प्राप्तः किम् पुनः ते महाबलाः |

न हि प्रकृष्टाः प्रेष्यन्ते प्रेष्यन्ते हि इतरे जनाः || ५-६८-२२
verses.translation:
"'In them, some of the monkeys are superior to me and some are even equal to me. No one 
in the vicinity of Sugreeva is inferior to me. When I have arrived here, why to talk about those mighty 
ones? Generally, superior ones are not sent for errands. Only others are indeed sent'."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अलम् परितापेन देवि मन्युः व्यपैतु ते |

एक उत्पातेन ते लन्काम् एष्यन्ति हरि यूथपाः || ५-६८-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. devi = O Seetha the princess!; tat = that is why; alam 
= enough; paritaapena = of your lamentation; te shokaH = (Let) your sorrow; vyapaitu 
= be removed; te = those; hari yuuthapaaH = leaders of monkeys; eSyanti = 
will reach; laN^kaam = Lanka; eka utpaatena = by one single leap.
verses.translation:
"'O Seetha the princess! Enough of your lamentation. Take away your sorrow. Those leaders 
of monkeys will reach Lanka by one single leap'."

verses.sanskrit:
मम पृष्ठ गतौ तौ च चन्द्र सूर्याव् इव उदितौ |

त्वत् सकाशम् महाभागे नृ सिम्हाव् आगमिष्यतः || ५-६८-२४
verses.translation:
"Rama and Lakshmana, those two lions among men, also of great strength, ascending on 
my shoulders, can come to you, like the rising sun and the moon."

verses.sanskrit:
अरिघ्नम् सिम्ह सम्काशम् क्षिप्रम् द्रक्ष्यसि राघवम् |

लक्ष्मणम् च धनुष् पाणिम् लन्का द्वारम् उपस्थितम् || ५-६८-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. raaghavam = Rama; arighnam = the annihilator of enemies;
simha samkaasham = similar to a lion; lakSmaNam cha = and Lakshmana; dhanuS paaNim 
= wielding or bow in his hand; kSipram = will soon; upaasthitam = approach; laN^kaa 
dvaaram = the entrance gate of Lanka; drakSyasi = and you will see (them).
verses.translation:
"Rama, the annihilator of enemies, similar to a lion, and Lakshmana wielding a bow in 
his hand, will surely approach the entrance-gate of Lanka and you will see them soon."

verses.sanskrit:
नख दम्ष्ट्र आयुधान् वीरान् सिम्ह शार्दूल विक्रमान् |

वानरान् वानर इन्द्र आभान् क्षिप्रम् द्रक्ष्यसि सम्गतान् || ५-६८-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. drakSyasi = you can see; kSipram = soon; viiraan vaanaraan 
= the valiant monkeys; nakha damSTra aayudhaan = with their claws and tusks as weapons; 
simha shaarduula vikramam = having the prowess of lions and tigers; vaaraNendra bhaan 
= looking like lordly elephants; samgataan = coming together.
verses.translation:
"'You can see soon those valiant monkeys, with their claws and tusks as weapons, having 
the prowess of lions and tigers and looking like lordly elephants, coming here together'."

verses.sanskrit:
शैल अम्बुदन् निकाशानाम् लन्का मलय सानुषु |

नर्दताम् कपि मुख्यानाम् अचिरात् शोष्यसे स्वनम् || ५-६८-२७
verses.translation:
"'You will hear within a short time, the clamour of the excellent of monkeys resonating 
like a mountains and clouds, on the peaks of Mount Malaya in Lanka'."

verses.sanskrit:
निवृत्त वन वासम् च त्वया सार्धम् अरिम् दमम् |

अभिषिक्तम् अयोध्यायाम् क्षिप्रम् द्रक्ष्यसि राघवम् || ५-६८-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. drakSyasi = you will see; raaghavam = Rama; arim damam 
= the annihilator of enemies; nivR^itta vana vaasam = returned from his exile; abhiShiktam 
= and getting; anointed to the crown; ayodhyayaam = in Ayodhya; tvayaa saridham = 
along with you; kShipram = soon.
verses.translation:
"'You will see Rama, the annihilator of enemies, returning from exile, and getting anointed 
to the crown in Ayodhya, along with you soon'."

verses.sanskrit:
ततो मया वाग्भिः अदीन भाषिणी |

शिवाभिः इष्टाभिः अभिप्रसादिता |

जगाम शान्तिम् मम मैथिल आत्मजा |

तव अपि शोकेन तथा अभिपीडिता || ५-६८-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. tataH = thereafter; tava shokena api = though tormented with 
thought of your sorrow too; abhipiiDitaa = the tormented; maithila aatmajaa = Seetha;
abhiprasaadita = was soothened; mayaa = by me; adiina bhaaShiNii = through 
comforting words spoken; mama vaagbhiH = and by my words; shiivaabhiH = which were 
well disposed; iSTaabhiH = and agreeable; jagaama = obtained; shaantim = 
peace of mind.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter, soothened by me with comforting, well-disposed and agreeable words, Seetha, 
though tormented with thought of your grief, obtained peace of mind."
verses.commentary:
